manifest.title: Final Answer Error Rate Across Reasoning and Masking Strategies
manifest.caption: Compare the overall final answer error rate for all seven inference strategies: standard left-to-right chain-of-thought, budget-matched self-consistency, and the five masking/cloze-style variants. This is the primary results figure and establishes whether partial masking during inference or reasoning improves end-task performance over conventional decoding baselines.
manifest.label: fig:fig_main_results_all_conditions

manifest.title: Effect of Masking Design Choices
manifest.caption: Focus on the masking-based methods to isolate which design choices matter: backward cloze reconstruction, uncertainty-targeted selective repair, random span masking, forward-order cloze, and whole-rationale mask repair. This figure serves as the paper's ablation-style analysis by comparing targeted versus untargeted masking and backward versus forward reconstruction order.
manifest.label: fig:fig_masking_design_comparison

manifest.title: Where Masking Helps: Local, Non-local, and High-Confidence Errors
manifest.caption: Break down performance by error subtype using local recoverable subset error rate, non-local subset error rate, and high-confidence error rate across conditions. This figure tests whether partial masking mainly repairs locally recoverable reasoning failures, or whether it also reduces harder non-local and overconfident errors.
manifest.label: fig:fig_error_breakdown_by_subset

This is the primary results figure and establishes whether partial masking during inference or reasoning improves end-task performance over conventional decoding baselines.](charts/fig_main_results_all_conditions.png)

![Figure 2: Focus on the masking-based methods to isolate which design choices matter: backward cloze reconstruction, uncertainty-targeted selective repair, random span masking, forward-order cloze, and whole-rationale mask repair. This figure serves as the paper's ablation-style analysis by comparing targeted versus untargeted masking and backward versus forward reconstruction order.](charts/fig_masking_design_comparison.png)

![Figure 3: Break down performance by error subtype using local recoverable subset error rate, non-local subset error rate, and high-confidence error rate across conditions. This figure tests whether partial masking mainly repairs locally recoverable reasoning failures, or whether it also reduces harder non-local and overconfident errors.](charts/fig_error_breakdown_by_subset.png)
figure_descriptions:
## AVAILABLE FIGURES (embed in the paper)
The following figures were generated from actual experiment data. Reference them in the appropriate paper sections using markdown image syntax: `![Caption](charts/filename.png)`

**Figure 1** (`charts/fig_main_results_all_conditions.png`) — Final Answer Error Rate Across Reasoning and Masking Strategies
  Caption: Compare the overall final answer error rate for all seven inference strategies: standard left-to-right chain-of-thought, budget-matched self-consistency, and the five masking/cloze-style variants. This is the primary results figure and establishes whether partial masking during inference or reasoning improves end-task performance over conventional decoding baselines.
  Place in: **Results** section

**Figure 2** (`charts/fig_masking_design_comparison.png`) — Effect of Masking Design Choices
  Caption: Focus on the masking-based methods to isolate which design choices matter: backward cloze reconstruction, uncertainty-targeted selective repair, random span masking, forward-order cloze, and whole-rationale mask repair. This figure serves as the paper's ablation-style analysis by comparing targeted versus untargeted masking and backward versus forward reconstruction order.
  Place in: **Analysis** section

**Figure 3** (`charts/fig_error_breakdown_by_subset.png`) — Where Masking Helps: Local, Non-local, and High-Confidence Errors
  Caption: Break down performance by error subtype using local recoverable subset error rate, non-local subset error rate, and high-confidence error rate across conditions. This figure tests whether partial masking mainly repairs locally recoverable reasoning failures, or whether it also reduces harder non-local and overconfident errors.
  Place in: **Analysis** section


For each figure referenced, write a descriptive caption and discuss what the figure shows in 2-3 sentences.
